summary: Systematic review and meta-analysis comparing deep learning with health-care professionals at detecting diseases from medical imaging. Pooled estimates show roughly equivalent sensitivity and specificity, but very few studies used external validation or head-to-head clinician comparisons, and reporting quality was generally poor.

Constructs WikiCREM, a large unsupervised corpus of pronoun-disambiguation instances generated by masking repeated personal names in Wikipedia sentences. Language models fine-tuned on it improve coreference resolution across benchmarks such as GAP, DPR, and Winograd-style tasks without task-specific annotation.

Formalizes Kant's account of cognition as a computational architecture: making sense of sensory input means constructing a unified model of objects, properties, and causal laws satisfying Kantian synthetic-unity constraints, realized through logic-program synthesis.

Makes structure-exploiting combinatorial semi-bandit algorithms computationally tractable by reducing arm selection to submodular maximization. For matroid constraints, tailored approximation routines give the first efficient algorithms that use reward structure to improve regret, tightening the best efficient gap-free bound by a factor sqrt(m)/log m.

Addresses retrieving images containing a query set of identities: per-face embeddings are aggregated into one compact fixed-length descriptor by a trained SetNet with GhostVLAD-style pooling, enabling efficient large-scale set retrieval; introduces the Celebrity Together dataset for evaluation.

Independent multi-agent RL in simulated three-level predator-prey ecosystems — a 3D physics engine with tens of agents and 2D grid worlds with thousands — produces emergent flocking and cross-species symbiotic coordination without any hand-crafted shaping rewards encouraging it.

Relates a neural phrase-structure parser's internal states to EEG recorded as people listened to the same literary text. More training data yields a better cognitive model only when training examples match the text's genre, consistent with humans adapting syntactic expectations to genre.

Combines Kalman temporal differences with the successor representation to learn a distribution over the SR, capturing uncertainty and covariances between long-term predictions. KTD-SR reproduces human-like partial transition revaluation without replay, unlike standard TD-SR, linking predictive and probabilistic accounts of animal learning.

Interprets dataset unfairness as unfair causal paths in the causal Bayesian network of the data-generating mechanism, revisiting the COMPAS risk-assessment debate. Argues fairness evaluation must consider unfairness patterns in training data, and shows CBNs help measure unfairness and design fair models.

Proposes an exact determinantal point process sampler whose one-time preprocessing costs n*poly(k) — sublinear in the kernel matrix size — and whose per-sample cost is poly(k), independent of n, making diversity-inducing subset sampling practical for very large datasets.

A segmental neural language model jointly discovers word-like units in unsegmented character streams, learns how words compose into sentences, and grounds word meanings in visual context. It out-predicts character LSTMs, segments competitively with Bayesian models, and improves further when visually conditioned.

Pythia, a sequence-to-sequence network, restores missing characters in damaged ancient Greek inscriptions, achieving roughly 30% character error rate versus 57% for human epigraphists; its ranked restoration hypotheses are intended as a search-space-narrowing aid for historians.

Aggregates multiple face images of a subject into one compact template descriptor using a NetVLAD extension with 'ghost' clusters that absorb low-quality faces, automatically down-weighting them. Sets state-of-the-art set-based face recognition on the IJB-B benchmark.

Human multi-armed bandit experiments show confidence in value beliefs arbitrates exploration versus exploitation: people explore more when belief confidence is low. Belief confidence also drives choice confidence, and individuals with greater metacognitive insight track environmental uncertainty better.

Extends recurrent neural network grammars to the unsupervised setting, learning latent constituency structure jointly with language modeling via amortized variational inference using a CRF parser as inference network. Achieves strong perplexity and competitive unsupervised parsing without annotated trees.

Schedules status updates over an error-prone channel to minimize long-term average age of information under a transmission budget, deriving the optimal policy for ARQ and structural results for HARQ, plus an average-cost reinforcement-learning algorithm for unknown channel statistics.

Shows the gradient noise scale, a simple statistic, predicts the largest useful data-parallel batch size across supervised and RL tasks: complex tasks with noisier gradients tolerate bigger batches. Offers an empirical model that systematizes compute/time tradeoffs in large-batch training.

Introduces CoinRun, a procedurally generated platformer that quantifies generalization in RL. Agents overfit to surprisingly large training sets, while deeper convolutional architectures and supervised-learning-style regularization — L2, dropout, data augmentation, batch norm — improve transfer to unseen levels.

Educational resource for deep reinforcement learning: clear, minimal implementations of core algorithms (VPG, TRPO, PPO, DDPG, TD3, SAC) plus tutorials, exercises, and curated paper lists designed to take newcomers to practitioner level.

An energy-based model learns spatial concepts such as near, above, between, and furthest from about five demonstrations of 2D point sets, supporting both recognition and generation of concept instances, and transfers concepts learned in 2D to a physics-based 3D robot.

POLO couples online trajectory optimization with an offline-learned value function ensemble: the value function extends the effective planning horizon, ensemble disagreement supplies exploration bonuses, and local planning stabilizes value learning, enabling rapid acquisition of simulated humanoid control without demonstrations.

Random Network Distillation rewards an RL agent with its error at predicting a fixed, randomly initialized network's features for each state, a cheap curiosity bonus for hard-exploration games. Combined with PPO, it exceeded average human performance on Montezuma's Revenge for the first time.

Proposes iterated amplification for AI safety: behaviors and goals beyond human scale are specified by decomposing tasks into simpler subtasks a human aided by AI assistants can handle, instead of labels or reward functions. Early experiments cover only toy algorithmic domains.

FFJORD builds continuous-time reversible generative models by estimating log-densities with Hutchinson's trace estimator on ODE-defined transformations, lifting normalizing flows' architectural restrictions. Achieves state-of-the-art exact-likelihood results on density estimation, image generation, and variational inference with one-pass sampling.

OpenAI Five lost both exhibition games against professional Dota 2 teams at The International 2018, though it maintained a good chance of winning through the first 20-35 minutes of each match — a public stress test of large-scale self-play RL.

First large-scale study of purely curiosity-driven RL, with no extrinsic rewards, across 54 environments: prediction-error objectives align surprisingly well with hand-designed game rewards. Random features often suffice but learned features generalize better, and stochastic 'noisy-TV' dynamics expose the approach's limits.

OpenAI Five won a best-of-three against a team of 99.95th-percentile Dota 2 players, four of them former professionals, before a live audience — a milestone for large-scale self-play reinforcement learning ahead of its appearance at The International.

Dactyl trains a five-fingered Shadow Hand to reorient a block using large-scale PPO entirely in simulation, with extensive domain randomization of physics and visuals enabling vision-based dexterous manipulation to transfer to the physical robot without real-world training.

Casts unsupervised skill discovery as variational autoencoding between latent contexts and trajectories, unifying objectives like VIC and DIAYN. Proposes VALOR, which decodes contexts from whole trajectories and grows the number of contexts through a curriculum, learning diverse behaviors without task rewards.

Glow is a flow-based generative model built on invertible 1x1 convolutions and actnorm, simplifying reversible architectures. It generates realistic high-resolution faces, supports exact-likelihood training with efficient sampling, and exposes latent directions for semantic attribute manipulation.
